text.content: Equation A: 3 × 5 = 15

text.content: Equation C: 3 × 5 = 20

text.content: "Four equations. Three are true — both sides have the same value. One is false. Which one is false?"

rich_text.plain_text: "Four equations. Three are true — both sides have the same value. One is false. Which one is false?"

text.content:
tool: multiple_choice  options: ["A", "B", "C", "D"]
"Which equation is false?"

rich_text.plain_text:
tool: multiple_choice  options: ["A", "B", "C", "D"]
"Which equation is false?"

text.content: ✅ Student selected C, correct — 3 × 5 = 20 is false

rich_text.plain_text: ✅ Student selected C, correct — 3 × 5 = 20 is false

text.content: "Right. 3 times 5 is 15, not 20. The sides don't match. And notice, the other equations are all true. The total is on the right in some and on the left in others, but both sides have the same value either way. That's what the equals sign checks."

rich_text.plain_text: "Right. 3 times 5 is 15, not 20. The sides don't match. And notice, the other equations are all true. The total is on the right in some and on the left in others, but both sides have the same value either way. That's what the equals sign checks."

text.content: "Let me show you how to check. I solve the left side: 3 times 5 is 15. The right side says 20. 15 does not equal 20. The sides don't match, so this equation is false. The answer is C. The equals sign checks if both sides have the same value."

text.content: "Let me show you. Books: I count 4 rows, 2 books in each row. Cups: 4 stacks, 2 cups in each stack. Butterflies: 4 circles, 2 butterflies in each circle. Every picture has the same structure: 4 groups of 2. That gives us 8 things total. The correct answers are 'All have 4 groups of 2' and 'All have 8 things.'"

rich_text.plain_text: "Let me show you. Books: I count 4 rows, 2 books in each row. Cups: 4 stacks, 2 cups in each stack. Butterflies: 4 circles, 2 butterflies in each circle. Every picture has the same structure: 4 groups of 2. That gives us 8 things total. The correct answers are 'All have 4 groups of 2' and 'All have 8 things.'"
